business.headline: Why your salary is losing the race against home pricesIndia's housing dream is facing its biggest affordability test as rising prices force buyers to choose between bigger debts, longer commutes and delayed dreams.

FDs, SCSS or mutual funds? The best way to invest Rs 50 lakh after retirementRetiring with Rs 50 lakh may sound reassuring, but the real challenge begins after retirement: how do you turn that lump sum into a steady monthly income without running out of money?

US Justice Department okays Paramount's $110 billion Warner Bros takeoverAfter an eight-month review, the DOJ concluded the proposed merger would strengthen competition across streaming, television and film, potentially benefiting consumers, workers and the broader entertainment industry.

SpaceX opens at $150 in strong Nasdaq debut, up 11% from IPO priceSpaceX surged more than 20 per cent in its Nasdaq debut, lifting its valuation above USD 2 trillion as investors piled into the world's biggest IPO. The stock opened at USD 150, signalling strong demand and easing concerns over its market debut.

Groceries, fuel, school fees: Is your monthly budget about to get tighter?School fees, petrol, vegetables, utility bills—the list of monthly expenses never seems to end. As inflation rises, many families are asking the same question: is their budget about to get even tighter?

Inflation rises to 3.93% for May as West Asia conflict pushes up fuel and food pricesFood inflation continued to move higher in May. It stood at 4.78%, up from 4.20% in April, showing that prices of food items are rising faster than they were a month earlier.

Markets rally on easing West Asia tensions, oil falling below $88The S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Salary mismatch to missed interest: 7 ITR mistakes that can trigger tax noticesFiling your ITR may seem straightforward, but even a minor reporting error can attract the attention of the tax department. Here are seven mistakes you should watch out for.

The world's biggest IPO starts trading today. Is Elon Musk's SpaceX overvalued?SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq after a record $75 billion IPO and a $1.75 trillion valuation. The debut has sharpened questions over valuation, Musk's control, xAI losses and broader market liquidity.

FD rates up to 8.10%: Check these small finance banks offering the highest returnsIn a world of rising uncertainty, guaranteed returns are becoming more valuable. Here's a look at the small finance banks offering FD rates as high as 8.10% this month.

Govt's new petrol, diesel order: Who is affected, and why was it introduced?The Centre has restricted industrial and commercial bulk fuel purchases from retail pumps. The move aims to protect pump supplies for ordinary consumers amid a wide retail-bulk price gap.

Earning Rs 20 lakh? Claim these tax benefits under the new tax regimeThink the new tax regime leaves no room for tax savings? If you earn Rs 20 lakh a year, you may be surprised to learn that several tax-friendly benefits are still available.
Gold demand picks up despite PM's appeal to avoid buyingAt the time of writing at 11.45 am, MCX gold was trading at Rs 1,49,942, up Rs 1,021, while silver stood at Rs 2,42,232, higher by Rs 2,579.

India may let fiscal deficit widen as US-Iran war raises oil costsIndia may allow its fiscal deficit to widen to 4.8% of GDP as the Iran war raises oil-linked costs. The move reflects pressure from higher subsidies, costlier imports and possible spending cuts.

Inflation fears return: Will Indians have to spend more on everyday items?Rising global oil prices and stronger US inflation are reviving concerns over India's household costs. Economists and the RBI say risks are building even without an immediate inflation shock.

Sensex jumped 1,700 points: 3 reasons why stock market rose todayThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Markets surge as crude oil falls below $90 amid hopes of US-Iran breakthroughThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 873.88 points, or 1.18%, to 74,706.43 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 climbed 236.40 points, or 1.02%, to 23,398.00.

World's first trillionaire Elon Musk is now richer than Taiwan after SpaceX IPOElon Musk became the world's first trillionaire after SpaceX's record IPO lifted his holdings. The milestone pushed his fortune past Taiwan's economy and widened the gap with every other billionaire.

Centre restricts bulk industrial fuel purchases at petrol pumpsThe government has barred industrial, commercial and institutional users from buying petrol and diesel at retail pumps. The move seeks to prevent diversion, curb shortages and protect supplies for ordinary vehicle owners.

US drug agency puts Dabur products on import alert after inspection flags lapsesThe US FDA has placed drugs manufactured at Dabur India's Dadra and Nagar Haveli factory under an import alert after an inspection found data integrity and maintenance lapses.

entertainment.headline: Orry calls Dhruv Rathee anti-national, says I just don't like the guy Orry criticised Dhruv Rathee in a podcast, calling him anti-national. The social media influencer said he does not like the YouTuber and questioned his content choices.

Ram Charan, Upasana share first glimpse of their newborn twins: Heart is full Ram Charan and Upasana Konidela shared the first glimpse of their newborn twins in an Instagram post. The post also introduced Shivram and Anveera Devi alongside elder daughter Klin Kaara.

Matt Damon eager to return as Jason Bourne: We're always looking for a new story Matt Damon has said he is open to returning for another Bourne film. His remarks suggest the makers are still searching for a fresh story for the franchise.

Gadar 3 in the works: Director Anil Sharma reveals when filming may begin Director Anil Sharma shared a fresh update on Gadar 3 as the blockbuster franchise featuring Sunny Deol and Ameesha Patel marks 25 years. He also hinted at what needs to happen before the film finally goes on floors.

Sonam Kapoor's 41st birthday dump features an adorable glimpse of son Rudralokh Sonam Kapoor shared photographs from her birthday celebration, including a glimpse of her second son, Rudralokh. The post offered a quiet look at an intimate family gathering with close friends and relatives.

Is Buchi Babu Sana's next film with Mahesh Babu? Here's what Peddi director said At the Peddi success meet, director Buchi Babu responded cautiously to rumours of a film with actor Mahesh Babu. His measured reply has kept speculation alive as Mahesh Babu remains busy with SS Raajamouli's Varanasi.

Shahid-Kriti-Rashmika's Cocktail 2 gets A certificate, runtime revealed Cocktail 2 has been cleared by the CBFC with an 'A' certificate ahead of its June 19 release. The rating marks Kriti Sanon's first adult-rated film, Rashmika Mandanna's second in Hindi after Animal, and Shahid Kapoor's fifth overall.

Inside Disha Patani's low-key birthday with Mouni Roy and Krishna Shroff Mouni Roy shared photos and videos from Disha Patani's birthday celebration, which gave fans a glimpse of the intimate party. Stebin Ben and Krishna Shroff also joined the celebrations.

Chiranjeevi admits he's jealous of Ram Charan's Peddi role: Little hard to accept Megastar Chiranjeevi expressed pride and a tinge of jealousy over son Ram Charan's film Peddi, praising both the performance and its inspiring sports drama theme. He revealed that Peddi fulfilled a long-held desire of his own to star in such a film.

Kartik Aaryan marks 2 years of Chandu Champion, hints at a comeback Kartik Aaryan marked two years of Chandu Champion with a heartfelt note reflecting on the film and his transformation. The actor thanked director Kabir Khan and the team while promising that champions will be back.

Kunal Kamra takes a sharp jibe at Pranit More's apology over Rs 370 biryani row Kunal Kamra mocked Pranit More's apology as the Rs 370 biryani row from his show continued. The exchange has kept attention on Himanshu Jangra's remark, the backlash and More's response.

No shortcuts: Huma Qureshi hard launches Rachit Singh with emotional note Actor Huma Qureshi shared photos of Rachit Singh and praised his long journey in a warm Instagram post. His playful reply added fresh buzz to months of relationship and wedding speculation.

Vijay-Rashmika honour students in Telangana, offer scholarships to 180 rankholders Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna visited Telangana's Thummanpet to honour meritorious government school students from Achampet Mandal. The event linked a family homecoming with a new scholarship push for 180 students from the region.

Ikka: Sunny Deol, Akshaye Khanna's courtroom drama set for July release on Netflix Sunny Deol and Akshaye Khanna are set to face off in Siddharth P Malhotra's courtroom drama Ikka, which is set to premiere on Netflix. The film pairs a shared past with a legal battle that tests family, principle and belief.

Sweet Magnolias Season 5 review: Serenity still knows how to soothe the soulRelease Date:Thu Jun 11, 2026 Sweet Magnolias returns to Serenity for a quieter fifth season centred on Maddie, Dana Sue and Helen. The new episodes lean on friendship and emotional maturity even as familiar flaws remain.

Kajol, Juhi Chawla tease Aamir Khan at Lagaan event: He has great taste in women Actors Kajol and Juhi Chawla playfully roasted Aamir Khan at the 25th anniversary of Lagaan over his taste in women. Their banter drew laughter at the venue, leaving Aamir visibly shy.

Surbhi Jyoti, husband Sumit Suri welcome first child, a baby girl Television actor Surbhi Jyoti and her husband Sumit Suri have welcomed their first child, a baby girl. The couple announced their daughter's birth on social media as friends and fans sent their wishes.

Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata vs Main Vaapas Aaunga Day 2: Kangana trails behind Diljit Kangana Ranaut's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata and Diljit Dosanjh's Main Vaapas Aaunga continued their theatrical run on Day 2. The Diljit-led romance drama stayed ahead after both films opened to a subdued box office response.

Why is an Indian in first class? Zakir Khan recalls racist treatment on flight A resurfaced podcast clip of comedian Zakir Khan has reignited conversations about racism faced by Indians abroad. In the viral video, he recalls being questioned while travelling first class and shares how he responded to the incident with humour.

general news.headline: US and Iran reach peace agreement, Trump announces Strait of Hormuz reopeningPakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif first announced that the United States and Iran had reached a peace deal ending military operations. Shortly afterward, US President Donald Trump confirmed the agreement, declaring the deal complete and announcing the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the removal of the US naval blockade. The agreement is scheduled to be formally signed in Switzerland on June 19.

Rebel TMC MPs to merge with Nationalist Citizens Party, to back NDAThe latest political twist comes hours after the rebel faction regrouped at Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla's residence in Delhi following a meeting with Union Minister Bhupender Yadav. According to sources, the rebel TMC MPs opted to associate themselves with the party due to legal complications in forming a separate parliamentary bloc.

Cote D'Ivoire vs Ecuador Live: Kick-off in PhiladelphiaCote D'Ivoire (CIV) vs Ecuador (ECU) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Kick-off in Philadelphia as Ivory Coast and Ecuador begin their Group E clash. Both teams are settling into the contest with qualification points already at a premium.

Bibi has no f**king judgment: Trump says he was 'so pissed' over Beirut strikeWhile Trump has repeatedly insisted that the agreement would be signed on Sunday, a day that coincides with his 80th birthday, Iran has said no final decision has been taken on the framework and that discussions remain ongoing at the political, legal and technical levels.

Woman, 21, falls 130 feet to death after bungee jump crew forgets to attach ropeMaria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas died after falling from a bridge during a bungee jump in Sao Paulo when the safety cord was allegedly not attached. The incident prompted the arrest of six people and intensified scrutiny of safety standards at the site.

Dutch pay for shutting up shop as Japan rescue deserved pointJapan came from behind twice to earn a deserved 2-2 draw against the Netherlands, punishing a Dutch side that retreated after taking control of their Group F clash.

India strangle Pakistan with spin to begin T20 World Cup with statement winWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs PAK: Harmanpreet Kaur's India made a stupendous start to their campaign after hammering Pakistan by 64 runs on Sunday. Smriti Mandhana laid the platform with a fifty, after which Deepti Sharma took five wickets.

Who is Livano Comenencia? Curacao hero scores historic World Cup goal vs GermanyFIFA World Cup 2026: Livano Comenencia scored Curacao's first World Cup goal as the debutants drew level against Germany in Houston. The strike triggered wild celebrations and marked a landmark moment for the tournament's smallest qualified nation.

India's Harmanpreer Kaur refuses to shake hands with Pakistan captainThe no handshake policy between India and Pakistan continued in the Women's T20 World Cup 2026 after Harmanpreet Kaur won the toss and opted to bat first in Edgbaston. Indian cricket teams have maintained the policy since 2025.

Germany spoils Curacao's World Cup debut with 7-1 hammeringGermany overcame an early scare to demolish Curacao 7-1 in Houston in their opening contest on Sunday, June 14. A brace from Kai Havertz ensured that Germany started off on the right note in the tournament.

How rebel TMC MPs' merger with little-known party could benefit NDANineteen rebel TMC MPs met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla to press their merger claim with Tripura-based Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) and seek separate seating. The move has opened a legal contest over disqualification, party legitimacy and numbers for key Bills.

12 killed as skydiving plane crashes after takeoff in US state of MissouriTwelve people, including 11 skydivers and a pilot, were killed after a Pacific Aerospace P750 crashed shortly after taking off from Butler Memorial Airport in Missouri. The FAA said the NTSB will lead the investigation into the cause of the crash.

Mumbai bakes: Why is the city feeling so hot at night, and where is monsoon?Mumbai recorded its warmest June night as the minimum temperature stayed above 30°C amid a stalled monsoon. The lack of sustained rain and moisture-heavy Arabian Sea winds has kept the city unusually hot after sunset.

How Vastushastra helped Iranis build Mumbai's cafe cultureWhat if we told you one of Mumbai's most enduring food cultures was born because Vastushastra once deemed their locations inauspicious? The story of the Irani cafes is not just about chai and brun-maska and old Bombay's charm, but about Irani migrants who turned Bombay's unwanted corner plots into some of its most unforgettable cultural spaces.

There should be no more attacks: Trump warns Israel, says Iran deal within reachUS President Donald Trump and Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif had earlier said a deal was expected to be signed on Sunday, but Tehran signalled uncertainty over the timing even before the Beirut strike.

Theatre of absurd: Kapil Sibal slams rebel TMC MPs' merger moveAccording to sources, the rebel TMC MPs decided to associate themselves with the Nationalist Citizens Party of India due to legal complications in forming a separate bloc.

Gun Gurnoor: 6ft 5in pacer makes a mighty impression in DharamsalaIndia fast bowler Gurnoor impressed on his debut for India on Saturday, June 13. Playing against Afghanistan, Gurnoor bowled at high pace, justifying his fast tracking to the Indian cricket team.

Ready to help end Ukraine conflict: Trump tells Putin, says Iran deal is nearDonald Trump told Vladimir Putin that ending the Ukraine conflict was vital and said he was ready to help. The call also covered Iran talks, with Trump saying a US-Iran agreement was close.

Live: Trump says he will ask Iran not to attack Israel in response to Lebanon strikesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump announced on Saturday that a landmark agreement between the United States and Iran is scheduled to be signed on Sunday. He claimed the deal would pave the way for the immediate reopening of the strategic Strait of Hormuz and mark a fundamental shift in diplomatic relations between the two long-standing adversaries. In a statement posted on his Truth Social platform, Trump drew a sharp comparison between his upcoming accord and the previous agreement brokered under former President Barack Obama. He emphasised that, unlike the framework established during his predecessor's tenure, this newly negotiated deal will strictly ensure that Iran is completely barred from developing nuclear weapons. To catch all the live updates, follow our live blog indiatoday.in.

Three killed, 29 injured as runaway truck overturns bus in MP's KatniAn out-of-control truck hit a car and a bus before crashing into a bridge in Katni, leaving three dead and 29 injured. Rescue teams used a crane to free trapped passengers as the state announced compensation for the victims.

Ram Charan, Upasana share first glimpse of their newborn twins: Heart is fullRam Charan and Upasana Konidela shared the first glimpse of their newborn twins in an Instagram post. The post also introduced Shivram and Anveera Devi alongside elder daughter Klin Kaara.

F1 2026: Lewis Hamilton ends Ferrari wait, storms to Barcelona GP winLewis Hamilton took his first Ferrari victory at the Barcelona Grand Prix after a late virtual safety car swung strategy decisively his way. Kimi Antonelli's late retirement handed George Russell second and produced a rare all-British podium.

What's in Iran deal US President Trump says he is ready to sign Live UpdatesSpeaking on condition of anonymity, a senior Iranian official said a draft memorandum of understanding covers a range of issues, including Iran's nuclear programme, the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and US sanctions relief.

Macron shares 'nice' selfie with PM Modi as India, France agree to double tradeModi and Macron held bilateral talks in Nice after the French President shared a selfie captioned 'nice'. The meeting produced a trade target, an innovation roadmap and wider cooperation across defence, technology and education.

sports.headline: What's in that notebook? Fans think Japan coach has a Death Note Japan coach Hajime Moriyasu's tactical changes against the Netherlands reignited the famous Death Note meme, leaving many fans wondering about the story behind the joke.

Cote D'Ivoire vs Ecuador Live: Big Group K clash in Philadelphia Cote D'Ivoire (CIV) vs Ecuador (ECU) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Germany have already thrown down the gauntlet in Group E, thrashing Curacao 7-1 to storm to the top of the standings. Now it's over to Ivory Coast and Ecuador in Philadelphia, with both sides knowing a win would keep pace with the early group leaders.

Dutch pay for shutting up shop as Japan rescue deserved point Japan came from behind twice to earn a deserved 2-2 draw against the Netherlands, punishing a Dutch side that retreated after taking control of their Group F clash.

A nod to Hanuman ji, Deepti Sharma scripts another ICC event masterclass Deepti Sharma dragged India past Pakistan with a decisive spell in the Women's T20 World Cup. The performance underlined her habit of peaking on the ICC stage despite lean form coming in.

Curacao's World Cup dream lives on despite crushing Germany defeat FIFA World Cup 2026: Curacao coach Dick Advocaat urged his players to stay positive after a 7-1 defeat to Germany, saying the nation's historic World Cup debut remains a moment of pride.

Germany spoils Curacao's World Cup debut with 7-1 hammering Germany overcame an early scare to demolish Curacao 7-1 in Houston in their opening contest on Sunday, June 14. A brace from Kai Havertz ensured that Germany started off on the right note in the tournament.

Who is Livano Comenencia? Curacao hero scores historic World Cup goal vs Germany FIFA World Cup 2026: Livano Comenencia scored Curacao's first World Cup goal as the debutants drew level against Germany in Houston. The strike triggered wild celebrations and marked a landmark moment for the tournament's smallest qualified nation.

Richa Ghosh as opener? Harmanpreet in awe of India batter after Pakistan cameo Women's T20 World Cup, IND vs PAK: Richa Ghosh starred with a blistering 34 off 17 balls to power India to a commanding 64-run win over Pakistan in their opening game on Sunday.

India strangle Pakistan with spin to begin T20 World Cup with statement win Women's T20 World Cup, IND vs PAK: Harmanpreet Kaur's India made a stupendous start to their campaign after hammering Pakistan by 64 runs on Sunday. Smriti Mandhana laid the platform with a fifty, after which Deepti Sharma took five wickets.

NED 2-2 JAP, FIFA World Cup Highlights: Japan steal one point against the Dutch Netherlands vs Japan, FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: (NED 2-2 JAP) Japan earned a huge point in Group F after twice coming from behind to hold the Netherlands to a thrilling 2-2 draw in Arlington. Goals from Ayase Ueda and late substitute Koki Ogawa cancelled out strikes from Donyell Malen and Crysencio Summerville in a pulsating second half.

Manpreet Singh equals Dilip Tirkey's India record as caps controversy ends Manpreet Singh matched Dilip Tirkey's Indian record of 412 international caps against the Netherlands in Rotterdam. The appearance ends months of speculation over whether he would be stopped from surpassing the mark.

F1 2026: Lewis Hamilton ends Ferrari wait, storms to Barcelona GP winLewis Hamilton took his first Ferrari victory at the Barcelona Grand Prix after a late virtual safety car swung strategy decisively his way. Kimi Antonelli's late retirement handed George Russell second and produced a rare all-British podium.

How Ronaldo, Portugal plan to pay tribute to Diogo Jota at World Cup 2026Portugal will wear commemorative wristbands for Diogo Jota during their World Cup campaign. Ronaldo and the rest of the team will be wearing the wristbands during matches as they chase their first World Cup.

Bangladesh survive Netherlands scare to begin Women's T20 World Cup with record chaseBangladesh overcame a spirited challenge from the Netherlands to begin their Women's T20 World Cup campaign with a six-wicket win, completing their highest-ever successful chase in the tournament.

Gun Gurnoor: 6ft 5in pacer makes a mighty impression in DharamsalaIndia fast bowler Gurnoor impressed on his debut for India on Saturday, June 13. Playing against Afghanistan, Gurnoor bowled at high pace, justifying his fast tracking to the Indian cricket team.

India's Harmanpreer Kaur refuses to shake hands with Pakistan captainThe no handshake policy between India and Pakistan continued in the Women's T20 World Cup 2026 after Harmanpreet Kaur won the toss and opted to bat first in Edgbaston. Indian cricket teams have maintained the policy since 2025.

UFC Freedom 250 at White House: Preview, livestreaming details and moreUFC Freedom 250 will stage two title fights and a stacked card on the White House South Lawn. The venue makes it a first for the promotion, turning a major fight night into a historic spectacle.

Dhiraj, Kumkum shine as India shock Korea to win World Cup archery goldDhiraj Bommadevara and Kumkum Mohod produced a fearless display to upset Olympic champions South Korea 5-1 and win the recurve mixed team gold at the Archery World Cup Stage 3 in Antalya.

Cricket who? FIFA World Cup celebrations in Australia go viralA video of football fans celebrating Australia's 2-0 win over Turkey went viral on Sunday, June 14. After seeing the incredible celebrations, many asked a simple question: Is cricket even popular outside of India?

No Spanish at World Cup? Hakimi, Vini Jr silenced by bizarre FIFA media rulesAchraf Hakimi and Vinicius Junior were discouraged from speaking Spanish during World Cup media sessions ahead of Brazil's opener against Morocco. Both players were left stunned by the media rules from FIFA.

IND vs PAK Highlights: India's spin web chokes Pakistan to start with big winIndia (IND-W) vs Pakistan (PAK-W) Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: Deepti Sharma's five-wicket haul helps India hammer Pakistan by 64 runs to start their campaign with a big win. India move to second in Group A, behind Australia.

Mohsin Naqvi set to bring radical changes to PCB amid Pakistan cricket's strugglesPCB chairman Mohsin Naqvi plans to expand the cricket advisory panel by adding more former Pakistan players, believing greater cricketing expertise will lead to better decisions and improved results.

Neeraj Chopra named in India's CWG athletics squad despite injury woesThe Athletics Federation of India named India's 32-member Commonwealth Games squad on Sunday, June 14. Headlined by sprinters Gurindervir Singh and Animesh Kujur, the squad also includes Neeraj Chopra, who is currently recovering from his back injury.

FIFA World Cup 2026: Turkiye defeated by Australia's height in VancouverFIFA World Cup 2026: Australia beat Turkiye 2-0 in Vancouver with a ruthless counterattacking display and a defensive line Turkiye could not breach. The result exposed the limits of star power against structure and delivered the first major upset of the FIFA World Cup 2026.

World Cup: Germany coach Nagelsmann confirms Neuer's return for Curacao gameGermany coach Julian Nagelsmann confirmed on Saturday that veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer will start against Curacao on Sunday, marking his return to international football after nearly two years and his fifth FIFA World Cup appearance.

From refugee camp to FIFA World Cup glory: Australia's Irankunda rewrites historyFIFA World Cup 2026: Australia beat Türkiye 2-0 in Vancouver as Nestory Irankunda scored from a rapid counterattack. The goal made him Australia's youngest World Cup scorer and sharpened the scale of his rise from Kigoma refugee camp to the elite game.

Indian-origin footballer debuts as Australia cause mega-upset in FIFA World CupIndian-origin winger Nishan Velupillay made his FIFA World Cup debut as Australia beat Turkiye 2-0 in their Group D opener. The result gave the Socceroos an early edge in the qualification race and marked another step in Velupillay's rapid rise.

Australia stun Turkiye 2-0 in Vancouver to deliver first World Cup upsetFIFA World Cup 2026: Australia beat Turkiye 2-0 in Vancouver through sharp counter-attacking and stubborn defending. The result jolts Group D, pushes the Socceroos towards the top and leaves Turkiye needing a response.

England's stolen football gear worth Rs 17 lakh recovered 24 hours after theftEngland football team's stolen gear, reportedly worth around Rs 17 lakh, was recovered by Kansas City police within 24 hours of the theft. Two men from Texas have been charged with felony counts by authorities.

The first FIFA World Cup memory that stayed with our newsroomNot every World Cup memory is shaped by what happened on the pitch. For members of India Today's News Desk, the tournament is also about the people, moments and emotions that turned football's biggest stage into something deeply personal. Comment down below about your first World Cup memory.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
